subject: UX & Research
title: Brain-Computer Interfaces
description: A short lesson to help you apply Brain-Computer Interfaces.

hook.text: In 2024, Neuralink's first human patient played chess on a computer using only his thoughts. No hands. No voice.

buildup.text: Brain-computer interfaces translate neural signals into digital commands. Think 'move cursor left' and it moves.

discovery.text: BCIs restore agency to paralyzed patients. A person who can't move can now type, browse, and communicate.

twist.text: Consumer BCIs are decades away. Current devices require surgery, are low-bandwidth, and only work for simple commands.

climax.text: If BCIs scale, the interface vanishes. Thought becomes input. The design challenge becomes parsing intention.

punchline.text: Thought as input. BCIs could make every other interface obsolete.

quiz.question: What is the primary current use case for brain-computer interfaces?
options: Restoring communication and control for paralyzed patients
